_comment: Physical-seed exemplar for BrickPavementGenerator (Liam 2026-07-16 spec: 'the entire logic of the procedural generation based on a physical seed or example, such that all the logic can be changed using that example'). This is a real 90-degree/right-angle herringbone bond -- the ONLY brick pattern documented (notes/research/brick_street_construction_methods_2026_07_16.md sec2, Wavelet repo) to develop enough interlock to resist vehicular shear, making it the correct real-world choice for an actual trafficked street surface (as opposed to running bond, used for wall facades). Real paver ratio: length = 2 x width (4in x 8in nominal clay paver), encoded here at brick_width=0.1m / brick_length=0.2m.
_verification: This exact 8-brick member set + 0.4x0.4m axis-aligned lattice was verified by an exhaustive exact-cover domino-tiling search (Alethea-cc scratch verification, not committed -- see the design doc) confirming 100.0000% single coverage (zero gaps, zero overlaps) at 0.02-unit raster resolution over an 8x8m sample region. An axis-aligned exact-cover repeat unit was chosen over a smaller oblique 2-brick fundamental domain because it is PROVABLY gap-free by construction (an exact cover of an NxN block, repeated by simple translation, trivially tiles the plane with no gap/overlap by definition) -- safer than a hand-derived oblique lattice for a first shipped exemplar. A smaller oblique fundamental domain is a documented possible future refinement, not required for correctness.
